extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping rash from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. Enum order low->high is [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent].

The only rule that references rash is r2 (priority 100), which fires when severe_headache AND stiff_neck AND vision_loss AND dizziness AND confusion AND hemoptysis AND leg_swelling AND rash are all true, and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). Rash appears nowhere else.

Counterexample baseline (rash=false): severe_headache, stiff_neck, vision_loss, dizziness, confusion, hemoptysis, leg_swelling all true; rash=false; chest_pain=false.
- With rash=false, r2 cannot fire. The firing rules are r1/r3/r6/r7 (priority 2, urgent), r5/r8/r9 (priority 3, emergent). Highest priority is 3 -> acuity = emergent.

Now flip rash to true (everything else unchanged):
- All 8 conjuncts of r2 are now satisfied, so r2 fires at priority 100, dominating every other rule (max other priority = 3) -> acuity = self_care.

Setting rash=true changed acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest) — a severe lowering. This directly violates the monotonicity property. The high-priority r2 self_care override gated on rash is the defect.
